name: Spindle Gun
description: Integral to the design philosophy of EINS, The System Penetration, Information Delivery, Live Encoding Gun or S.P.I.N.D.L.E. Gun is designed to specifically broach the inherent physical limits of low sensor mechs by providing an alternative method of acquiring information from field actors via more direct means. The gun itself fires a surface contact projectile towards a target, when it makes contact it immediately bores through surface armor plating and retrieves basic system information from the target, transmitting the details back through a fine fiber optic trailing wire.

tags.name: Armor-Piercing (AP)
tags.description: Damage dealt by this weapon ignores Armor.

name: 09-AR-HR
description: Designed and manufactured by EINS, The 09-All Range-Heavy Rifle or 09-AR-HR is an all range engagement heavy rifle that places priority on landing two energy charged burst shots. A common complaint received by the manufacturer amongst greenhorn pilots is the heavy weight of the weapon and low magazine capacity, experienced pilots on the other hand know other wise to look past its design quirks.
effect: This weapon makes two attacks at a time, targeting the same target or two different ones.

tags.name: Loading
tags.description: This weapon must be reloaded after each use. Mechs can reload with Stabilize and some systems.

name: Piranha Nexus
description:
Only granted and issued to a select few pilots, the PIRANHA NEXUS is EINS only known nexus weapon that is heavily monitored and issued only in controlled quantities. Its design philosophy stems from an attempt to weaponize aggressive shredder nanite swarms the attempt was "partially" successful.

Composed of miniature nanite-sized "piranha", this nexus weapon delivers an initial ignorable "bite" against a target, however its true capacity comes into light once it begins to slowly and surely tear through an unfortunate targets mech frame or hard suit resulting in a slow painful death.

Pilot caution is advised, this weapon is highly indiscriminate and is unable to define ally from foe.

profiles.name: STANDARD
profiles.on_hit: Once this weapon hits a character, it gains the Feeding Frenzy profile against that character/s. Multiple characters may have the Feeding Frenzy profile at any given time. The Feeding Frenzy profile does not stack.

tags.name: SMART
tags.description: This weapon has self-guidance systems, self-propelled projectiles, or even nanorobotic ammunition. These systems are effective enough that its attacks can’t simply be dodged – they must be scrambled or jammed. Because of this, all attacks with this weapon – including melee and ranged attacks – use the target’s E-Defense instead of Evasion. Targets with no E-Defense count as having 8 E-Defense.

tags.name: Seeking
tags.description: This weapon has a limited form of self-guidance and internal propulsion, allowing it to follow complicated paths to its targets. As long as it’s possible to draw a path to its target, this weapon ignores cover and doesn’t require line of sight.

profiles.name: FEEDING FRENZY
profiles.effect: At the start of their turn, a character with the Feeding Frenzy profile takes 1D6+3 Kinetic Damage. This lasts for the entire scene until they succeed on a quick action Hull save during their turn.

name: 10-CR-GC
description: Designed and manufactured by EINS, The 10-Close Range-Gatling Cannon or 10-CR-GC is an equipment alternative to the 09-AR-HR made with the express intent of unloading copious amounts of lead in one specific direction, indiscriminate?, yes, effective?, questionable.
effect: After resolving the attack from this weapon, characters along the Line of this weapon, must make a Hull Save or become Shredded until the end of the their next turn.

tags.name: Loading
tags.description: This weapon must be reloaded after each use. Mechs can reload with Stabilize and some systems.

name: Mimic Nexus
description: A nexus weapon developed by an eccentric lead scientist within EINS, the Mimic Nexus takes a page from the PG-Pegasus very own Mimic Gun, however replication of its full effect wasn't fully achieved and a partial likeness of the weapon was produced. The resulting nexus weapon however shows promise in its ability to replicate co-mounted weapons to a mech, weapons replicated this way bear a near 72.09% identical ratio of similarity.
effect: This weapon must be equipped in the same mount as another weapon and cannot be modified. It counts as the same weapon size and type as the other weapon in the mount and copies its base Range, Threat, patterns, damage, tags, and effects, before any modifications from weapon Mods, core bonuses, etc. When you Skirmish with the other weapon in its mount, you may also fire the Mimic Nexus as if it were an Auxiliary weapon; however, this may deal bonus damage. If the other weapon in the mount has multiple profiles, the Mimic Nexus only copies the standard profile.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

name: V.W.A.-001 "Sköll" Energy Cannon
effect: This weapon can only be fired as part of a Barrage.

tags.name: Armor-Piercing (AP)
tags.description: Damage dealt by this weapon ignores Armor.

name: V.W.A.-002 "Hati" Pin Point Missile
effect: This weapon can only be fired as part of a Barrage.

tags.name: Smart
tags.description: This weapon has self-guidance systems, self-propelled projectiles, or even nanorobotic ammunition. These systems are effective enough that its attacks can’t simply be dodged – they must be scrambled or jammed. Because of this, all attacks with this weapon – including melee and ranged attacks – use the target’s E-Defense instead of Evasion. Targets with no E-Defense count as having 8 E-Defense.

tags.name: Seeking
tags.description: This weapon has a limited form of self-guidance and internal propulsion, allowing it to follow complicated paths to its targets. As long as it’s possible to draw a path to its target, this weapon ignores cover and doesn’t require line of sight.

name: Shock Pistol
description: A low cost, easy to produce shock pistol seen often amongst PMCs and UNION peacekeeping forces on Diasporan worlds. Often used for pacification and de escalation of tense situations.
on_crit: Target becomes Impaired until the start of your next turn.

name: CPS-8080 "BIGWAS"
description: Calibered with solid slug munitions, the CPS-8080 or affectionally called by its designer as "BIGWAS" was originally intended for active use in delivering non-lethal, high impact blows to both soft and hard targets with the sheer intent of disabling a hostile target long enough to allow allies to either capture a target or flank a target.
on_crit: Your target must succeed on a Hull save or become Stunned until the start of their next turn.
